author: @stevetobias4890
text:
Looks good, where will the power switch go? I would have used a temperature meter instead of the amp meter. Getting an accurate overall amperage would be hard with the power supply outputting multiple 12v, 5v and 3.3v lines as well as multiple grounds. The temp could be taken from a sensor next to the CPU. Also where do the front ports come out (such as USB)
Overall an excellent video regardless of my questions above.

author: @MisterRorschach90
text: Now get some cheap copper water blocks for your cpu and gpu and replace the acrylic with wood grain, a bunch of copper or brass tubing, brass fittings, and a bunch of random sized copper heatsinks to replace all of the modern cooling on your motherboard including copper ram heat spreaders. Then make custom unsleeved copper cables for your psu. Then get metal fans and paint them the same copper color. Or if you’re feeling adventurous, mill your own fan blades out of wood, stain them, and make a copper fan housing, or vice versa, copper blades wood housing.  You could even mod your hdds to have a plexi front that shows off the platter. Remember to change all buttons for massive steampunk switches.

replies.author: @MisterRorschach90
replies.text: Also no cable management. Have all the cables stuffed in the back in a way that looks pleasing but chaotic.
